# mylesio

> Myles Liu's personal knowledge base. Frontend engineer, monitoring infra, AI agent practitioner.

## Notes

- [DeepSeek V4 and the Future of Context Engineering](/notes/deepseek-v4-context-engineering): Speculating on DeepSeek V4's architecture (Engram + mHC), and what context engineering looks like when the model can finally remember.
- [EndlessRun: Running Local Models for 24 Hours Straight](/notes/endlessrun-local-model-24h): A design for letting an 8B–26B edge model run complex tasks overnight using task decomposition, memory compression, and native tool calls.
- [Engineering Cybernetics Meets AI Harness](/notes/engineering-cybernetics-meets-ai-harness): How Qian Xuesen's 1954 control theory maps to the problem of making AI agents follow instructions reliably.
- [EyeGlow: Eye-Tracking Agent Interaction on MacBook](/notes/eyeglow-eye-tracking-agent): A design spec for using the MacBook front camera + eye direction + blink to replace mouse clicks when confirming AI agent prompts.
- [Fruit Fly Connectome × AI Agent Architecture](/notes/fruitfly-connectome-meets-agents): What the fruit fly's 139K-neuron brain wiring diagram teaches us about designing multi-agent communication topologies.
- [GenericAgent: A Self-Evolving Agent in 3K Lines](/notes/generic-agent-self-evolving-framework): How a minimal agent framework achieves 6x token efficiency through contextual information density maximization and autonomous skill crystallization.
- [Karpathy's LLM Wiki: Knowledge as Code](/notes/karpathy-llm-wiki): Instead of RAG-from-scratch every time, let the LLM continuously compile and maintain a living wiki. Notes on Karpathy's gist.
- [Multi-Agent Governance: Building Reliable AI Agent Teams](/notes/multi-agent-governance): How to organize multiple AI agents into reliable teams — roles, authorization, evaluation, prompt evolution, and the engineering principles behind it all.
- [Temporal Crystal: A Memory Compression and Retrieval Engine](/notes/temporal-crystal-memory-engine): An AI memory system with layered compression, hybrid retrieval, knowledge graphs, self-healing, and a dream engine inspired by biological sleep.
- [WorldMind: A Multi-Agent World Model Engine](/notes/worldmind-multi-agent-world-model): Architecture design for an autonomous system that perceives, remembers, reasons, predicts, and learns — starting with the GitHub open source ecosystem.

## Projects

- [prism](https://github.com/mylesio/prism): AI personal profile engine — parses conversations into structured personality insights
- [ant-protocol](https://github.com/mylesio/ant-protocol): Portable multi-agent governance protocol — task lifecycle, memory, queen orchestration
- [oasis-openclaw](https://github.com/mylesio/oasis-openclaw): Full agent intelligence injected into OASIS social simulation platform
- [worldmind](https://github.com/mylesio/worldmind): Multi-agent world model engine — perceive, reason, predict complex ecosystems
- [temporal-crystal](https://github.com/mylesio/temporal-crystal): Living memory for AI agents — memories that evolve, compress, crystallize
- [rumex](https://github.com/mylesio/rumex): Per-agent memory protocol — loop memory + task until done
- [claw-code](https://github.com/mylesio/claw-code): Multi-agent harness reference implementation

## About

- [About](/about): Background and links
